entity.summary: Australian centralized cryptocurrency exchange platform operated by Blockchain Global. ACX ceased operations abruptly in late 2019, leaving customers unable to withdraw assets, and Blockchain Global later entered voluntary administration and liquidation.
entity.notes: ACX is tracked as the exchange-facing product of Blockchain Global. Death date follows late-2019 customer-access collapse reporting; later administration and ASIC action provide follow-up context.

events.title: ACX operated under Blockchain Global
events.description: ACX operated as an Australian cryptocurrency exchange platform associated with Blockchain Global, formerly Bitcoin Group.
events.notes: Year-level date based on company and platform background references.

events.title: Customer withdrawals became inaccessible
events.description: ACX collapsed abruptly in late 2019, with customers unable to withdraw funds from the platform.
events.notes: Used as death_date because this marks the practical exchange-service collapse.

events.title: Blockchain Global entered administration and liquidation process
events.description: Blockchain Global later entered voluntary administration, with liquidators and regulators examining debts, creditor claims, and conduct of former directors.
events.notes: Follow-up legal and administration event supporting insolvency classification.

evidence.title: Blockchain Global background reference
evidence.notes: Used only for background cross-checking on Blockchain Global, Bitcoin Group, and ACX.

evidence.title: Collapse of crypto platform a cautionary tale
evidence.notes: Used for ACX collapse and customer-access context.

evidence.title: More than $50 million owed to creditors after collapse of Blockchain Global's cryptocurrency exchange
evidence.notes: Used for creditor debts, ACX exchange collapse, and customer-loss context.

evidence.title: ASIC sues former Blockchain Global director Liang Guo
evidence.notes: Regulatory follow-up on Blockchain Global's operation of ACX Exchange and alleged director duties.
